newsSince: Thesis intact but no genuinely new evidence since June 9. Nanya reiterates DRAM shortage through 2028, HBM rising to ~10% of DRAM output (from 7%) crowding out commodity DRAM, a new fab (45k wpm, 20k wpm 12-inch by 1H28) and custom AI-memory trials with results due 2H26 — all disclosed by March. The +25% move is a flow/cycle bounce.

sources.label: Nanya: DRAM shortage to last through 2028 (Taipei Times)
Nanya custom AI memory trial, results 2H26 (TrendForce)

verdicts.rankRationale: Memory-tier bottleneck (HBM crowding out commodity DRAM, shortage to 2028) is fully intact but well-broadcast; the +25% move since June 9 is a flow/cycle bounce, not new evidence. Custom AI-memory trial results are a 2H26 catalyst, not yet de-risked. Hold.
verdicts.bull: DRAM shortage to 2028, HBM crowd-out lifts commodity pricing; custom AI memory optionality H2 2026.
verdicts.neutral: Cyclical beneficiary, but not an HBM3E/HBM4 leader; alpha already broadcast.
verdicts.bear: Commodity-DRAM cycle and capex-timing risk; +120% YTD prices in much of the upcycle.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Custom AI memory trial-production results in 2H26.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Commodity memory cycle reversal / capex digestion.

verdicts.rankRationale: On the CPO lens this is a memory-cycle recovery story, not CPO/optical-interconnect alpha. No CPO-relevant news since June 9; the price spike reflects DRAM-cycle flow. Low directness keeps it near the bottom. Hold.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO relevance; pure memory-cycle play.
verdicts.bear: Commodity memory cycle + valuation after +120% YTD; not a packaging/optics bottleneck.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Commodity memory cycle; zero CPO directness.
